description: Color photograph, a still frame from law-enforcement body-worn camera footage with a wide-angle fish-eye distortion. The frame shows an indoor stairwell/lobby with a red brick wall, a metal handrail, and an orange traffic safety cone. In the foreground a heavyset man in a green athletic jersey bearing the white number '62', tan cargo pants, and lanyards around his neck stands holding white papers; his face is blurred. To the right are two other men: one in a grey trench coat and tie (face blurred) holding a phone, and a uniformed officer wearing a dark jacket with a yellow-and-blue shoulder patch reading 'POLICE'. Axon body-camera overlay text is burned into the top of the frame. The image has no graphic design, typography styling, agency seals, or slogan treatment. The accompanying tweet refers to an ICE arrest of a Jamaican national accused of attempted murder who had been bonded out by a Philadelphia police officer. Visible burned-in camera text: 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement', '2026-03-16 09:12:17 -0400', 'AXON BODY 4 D01A3441U'.
notable_text: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement; 2026-03-16 09:12:17 -0400; AXON BODY 4 D01A3441U
